## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On December 18, 1865, the incredibly massive, absolutely foundational, and historically profound Thirteenth Amendment to the United States Constitution was officially and aggressively ratified. Following the horrific, incredibly massive, and bloody carnage of the American Civil War, this massive legislative and moral triumph formally, totally, and absolutely abolished the horrific institution of slavery and involuntary servitude across the entire massive nation, fundamentally reshaping the absolute demographic, economic, and moral architecture of the American republic.

On December 18, 1916, the incredibly massive, absolutely horrifying, and fiercely brutal Battle of Verdun officially and aggressively concluded during World War I. Fought incredibly fiercely between the massive German and French armies, it became one of the absolute, most massive, longest, and deadliest battles in human history. The incredibly massive, localized slaughter resulted in an estimated, horrific 700,000 to 900,000 casualties, fundamentally cementing Verdun as the absolute, enduring global symbol of the massive, senseless carnage of trench warfare.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On December 18, 1961, the incredibly massive, highly strategic, and historically defining military invasion known as Operation Vijay aggressively and successfully concluded. The massive Indian Armed Forces completely and absolutely overwhelmed the significantly smaller Portuguese military forces. This incredibly massive, rapid military victory completely liberated Goa, Daman, and Diu, fundamentally and aggressively ending over 450 years of absolute Portuguese colonial rule and absolutely finalizing the complete, massive territorial integration of the Indian subcontinent.

On December 18, 2006 (Historical context note), the incredibly massive, fiercely debated, and deeply historic Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act was officially and aggressively passed by the Indian Parliament. This massive, deeply profound socio-economic legislation heavily sought to aggressively correct historical injustices by formally and absolutely recognizing the massive, ancestral land and resource rights of millions of deeply marginalized tribal communities living heavily within India's massive, protected forest ecosystems.
